level_signals: 4+ years of experience in building ML-powered and/or enterprise-facing products
Comp range $237,600–$297,000 base in SF/NY/SEA — consistent with Staff IC
Expected to work with executives and lead cross-functional teams

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Maintain a Top Secret security clearance
4+ years of experience in building ML-powered and / or enterprise-facing products
Strong understanding of generative AI technologies and their applications in public or large-scale private sector settings
Technical degree in computer science, engineering, or equivalent experience
Experience operating in a fast-paced environment with high ambiguity
Exceptional leadership, presentation, and communication skills with the ability to influence cross-functional teams
Data literacy and experience with data analytics

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience building infrastructure and tooling to develop and support agentic applications.
Experience working in startup environments building solutions for public sector / federal customers.
Understanding of public / federal networks, infrastructure, and deployment constraints.

scope.surface: Agentic AI platforms for public sector / national security use cases including agentic warfare, scenario planning, deep research, report generation, and Text2SQL intelligence applications
scope.business_model: government / public sector B2G, with ambition to commercialize into broader industry
primary_metrics_inferred: Successful delivery of national-security AI capabilities
Agency alignment and compliance with security mandates
core_responsibilities: Develop enterprise grade solutions that leverage cutting edge AI and AI agents to drive value for public sector customers
Work with executives at Scale and our customers to determine and execute the product strategy of the business
Own end-to-end product development by understanding customer pain points, defining product requirements, managing development, testing, and launches
Lead cross-functional teams including engineering, product design, operations, marketing, go-to-market and finance
Develop a point of view and execute on turning the solutions we build into scalable software that we can commercialize across the industry
Maintain a Top Secret security clearance

compensation.equity_form: equity grant subject to Board of Director approval

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($237,600–$297,000 base in primary markets)
Clear product surface with concrete examples of GenAI applications being built
Equity mentioned as part of compensation package
Benefits package clearly described including health, dental, vision, learning stipend, generous PTO
Scale has named government customers (Army, Air Force) giving product credibility
Explicit TS/SCI as nice-to-have rather than hard requirement (lower barrier vs peers)
red_flags: Top Secret security clearance is a hard requirement — eliminates most candidates without existing clearance
Prior military or government experience listed under 'Ideally you'd have' but the JD body treats it alongside unconditional requirements, creating classification ambiguity
Role involves classified environments; fully remote is unlikely and not stated
US citizenship almost certainly required for Top Secret clearance but not explicitly stated in JD — visa sponsorship and work auth are unclear from JD text alone
High buzzword density; some responsibilities are vague ('national-level decisions')
90-day waiting period policy noted for reapplication

fields_inferred_not_stated: location.type (inferred as onsite — classified/government work and clearance requirement strongly imply physical presence; not explicitly stated)
location.work_auth (inferred as us-citizenship-required — Top Secret clearance requires US citizenship; not explicitly stated in JD)
location.visa_sponsorship (inferred as no — Top Secret clearance requirement makes sponsorship implausible; not explicitly stated)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and commercialization language)
seniority.level (inferred from title 'Staff' and compensation range)
compensation.base_range_usd — using the higher SF/NY/SEA range as primary; DC/TX/CO/HI range is $213,400–$267,300
ambiguities: All requirements are listed under 'Ideally you'd have' which typically signals soft/preferred, but several (Top Secret clearance, years of experience, GenAI knowledge) read as hard requirements given the nature of the role. They have been classified as hard based on role context and language.
Top Secret clearance is listed as a hard must-have in the responsibilities ('Maintain a Top Secret security clearance') but TS/SCI is listed as a nice-to-have — both are captured accordingly.
Prior military or government experience appears under 'Ideally you'd have' but given the national security context is treated as a hard requirement here; flagged as ambiguous.
Two separate compensation ranges are provided for different geographies; $237,600–$297,000 is used as the primary range (SF/NY/SEA). Secondary range for DC/TX/CO/HI is $213,400–$267,300.
Location type is not explicitly stated; onsite inferred from clearance and classified network requirements.
